title: jasminum auriculatum
intro: Jasminum auriculatum, also known as the ear-drop jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Jasminum auriculatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
